div.p: Alexander, king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know us to have given, conceded, and by this our charter to have made firm, to Thomas d'Aunay, Burntscarthgreen, Auchencrieff, and Dargavel, by their own right boundaries by which William son of Derman held those lands, that is, the land which William son of the same William son of Derman has quitclaimed to us by staff and baton in our court at Dumfries for himself and his heirs. For the said Thomas d'Aunay and his own heirs to hold of us and our heirs in feu and heritage, in lands and waters, in meadows and pastures, in moors and marshes, in ponds and mills, and with all other things belonging by right to the foresaid lands, with gallows and ditch, with soke and sake, with toll and team, and infangtheif, freely and quitely, fully and honourably, by a quarter of the service of one knight. As witnesses: William del Bois, chancellor; Thomas of Stirling, archdeacon of Glasgow; Roger Avenel; Robert son of Robert of Roos; John of Maxwell; Peter de Valognes; William Bisset; William Avenel. At Ed[inburgh?] on the 29th day of September, in the tenth year of the lord king's reign.
